mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and corporate earnings visibility, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicate a supply-demand imbalance, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in GDPC1 and INDPRO fails to predict negative returns in > 70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2% (CPIAUCSL) prompts central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in industrial production (INDPRO) above 120 signals robust manufacturing activity, boosting corporate profits and predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to produce positive 5d returns in > 70% of high-INDPRO periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 2000 signals weak demand for construction, reducing related sector earnings and predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll gains (PAYEMS) above 150k combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signal a tightening labor market, increasing wage pressures and predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal fails to predict positive returns in > 70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in retail sales (RSAFS) above 350k signals strong consumer demand, boosting corporate revenues and predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of high-RSAFS periods.
